coredata.dc:title: Cerium-based metal organic framework (Ce-MOF) as corrosion inhibitor for API 5L X65 steel in CO2- saturated brine solution: XPS, DFT/MD-simulation, and machine learning model prediction

coredata.dc:creator: Anadebe, Valentine Chikaodili
Chukwuike, Vitalis Ikenna
Ramanathan, Sethupathy
Barik, Rakesh Chandra
coredata.dc:description: In this study, we report the synergistic effect of a metal ion/cluster (cerium nitrate hexahydrate) with an organic linker (2 methyl imidazole) as a nano-hybrid corrosion inhibitor for X65 steel in CO2 solution. To achieve this fit, a wide empirical study from wet chemical synthesis of Ce-MOF (cerium-metal organic framework), electrochemical method and surface analysis were considered. The empirical data obtained from the electrochemical studies were statistically analyzed via a machine learning model (adaptive neuro fuzzy inference system-ANFIS) considering multi input and single output function (MISO). The outcome revealed that Ce-MOF hindered the dissolution of ferrite and cementite phase of the steel in CO2 solution. The electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) revealed a significant rise in resistance to charge transfer with an increase in concentration of Ce-MOF. Polarization data indicated that Ce-MOF exhibited mixed-type inhibitor characteristics. The range of inhibition efficiencies were in the range of 97% and 95% at 0.15 wt% Ce-MOF for polarization and impedance studies, respectively. The theoretical study shows a flat adsorption orientation of Ce-MOF on the steel surface. Furthermore, the predictive capability of ANFIS model based on statistical norms; shows that the coefficient of determination (R2) is unity. From the statistical view point, much credibility was attributed to ANFIS model with robust description of the nonlinear interactions between the independent and dependent variables.

head.ce:title: Cerium-based metal organic framework (Ce-MOF) as corrosion inhibitor for API 5L X65 steel in CO2- saturated brine solution: XPS, DFT/MD-simulation, and machine learning model prediction

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In this study, we report the synergistic effect of a metal ion/cluster (cerium nitrate hexahydrate) with an organic linker (2 methyl imidazole) as a nano-hybrid corrosion inhibitor for X65 steel in CO2 solution. To achieve this fit, a wide empirical study from wet chemical synthesis of Ce-MOF (cerium-metal organic framework), electrochemical method and surface analysis were considered. The empirical data obtained from the electrochemical studies were statistically analyzed via a machine learning model (adaptive neuro fuzzy inference system-ANFIS) considering multi input and single output function (MISO). The outcome revealed that Ce-MOF hindered the dissolution of ferrite and cementite phase of the steel in CO2 solution. The electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) revealed a significant rise in resistance to charge transfer with an increase in concentration of Ce-MOF. Polarization data indicated that Ce-MOF exhibited mixed-type inhibitor characteristics. The range of inhibition efficiencies were in the range of 97% and 95% at 0.15 wt% Ce-MOF for polarization and impedance studies, respectively. The theoretical study shows a flat adsorption orientation of Ce-MOF on the steel surface. Furthermore, the predictive capability of ANFIS model based on statistical norms; shows that the coefficient of determination (R2) is unity. From the statistical view point, much credibility was attributed to ANFIS model with robust description of the nonlinear interactions between the independent and dependent variables.

ce:section.ce:para: Corrosion poses a great danger to the safety of multiphase oil and gas facilities leading to high cost of maintenance, loss of life and property (Askari, 2022; Anadebe et al., 2022a). The implications of ignoring corrosion may include environmental challenges and huge economic losses (Anadebe et al., 2022a). Specifically, CO2 corrosion known as sweet corrosion is the major risk factor endangering the oil and gas pipe lines (Wang et al., 2019). CO2 reacts with water to give a low pH solution (H2CO3) which is highly corrosive to steel. Unfortunately, the extraction processes in the oil and gas industries generate CO2, H2S and free water with a buildup of heavy corrosion scales.

Therefore, to ameliorate this industrial problem, the application of organic coatings can be employed, however, the addition of corrosion inhibitors into the corrosive environment is one of the most feasible practices to adopt without disruption of the production process, and thus prolong metals service life (Thakur et al., 2022; Alamry et al., 2022). Corrosion inhibitors have extensively been used in different corrosive environments under varying conditions (Shahmoradi et al., 2022). However, some of the inhibitors are not scalable due to their complex synthesis route, high cost and lack of sustainability (Khadom et al., 2015). On the contrary, eco-friendly based inhibitors meet the demand of green chemistry approach (non-toxic, cost-effective, biodegradable, easy and facile synthesis route) and can adequately avoid the disruption of industrial activities with extra advantage of less pollution (Macedo et al., 2019). In recent times, lots of green inhibitors have been designed to safeguard metals against CO2 corrosion (Rbaa et al., 2021; Zhao et al., 2020). However, it is seen that the challenging factor of green corrosion inhibitors is attributed to their relatively low inhibition performance due to poor solubility, difficulty in dispersion and optimization of synthesis conditions. It is still challenging to design a biocompatible green corrosion inhibitor with high inhibition efficiency. Therefore, adequate measures and professional techniques like capping, heteroatom doping and surface functionalization have been utilized to improve on the inhibition efficiency which is of great importance for eco-friendly corrosion inhibitors to enhance their industrial applicability, sustainability and reliability. In this regard, metal organic framework (MOF) comes with a great promise.

Zeolite imidazolate framework (ZIF) is a unique type of MOF having M: metal ion/clusters, and IM: imidazolate as organic linker (Sankar et al., 2020). They are described as emerging type of organic–inorganic (nanohybrid) material with significant crystalline texture. Many researchers have extensively evaluated MOFs as a sustainable material in many applications such as hydrogen storage, waste water treatment, CO2 reduction, drug delivery, sensor, solar cells/batteries and, recently in corrosion inhibition studies. Their multi-functionalities are favored by their unique surface loading capability, topology, low cost, biocompatibility and easy synthesis route (Cao et al., 2019; Haeri et al., 2022). Interestingly, the addition of strong electropositive metals to the organic framework promotes its inhibitory efficiency, while the organic linkers form the protective coverage on the metal surface (Fouda et al., 2021). However, few studies are available on hybrid corrosion inhibitors based on MOFs with predominant investigation in the area of mineral acid corrosion. Etaiw et al. (2011) reported a 3D network of silver based MOFs as corrosion inhibitor for carbon steel in 1 M HCl electrolyte. In another investigation by (Fouda et al., 2016), metal organic frameworks based on silver and nitrogen donors were proven to be a good corrosion inhibitor for Cu in HCl medium. Zhang et al. (2018) reported the use of hydrophobic MOFs such as ZIF 8 in anticorrosion coatings. Zafari et al. (2020) proposed ZIF-8 @ {Mo132} as a new MOF for carbon steel corrosion mitigation in acidic media.

Despite the success of zeolite and silver based MOFs, cerium MOF has not received attention as a corrosion inhibitor. The reports are only available on cerium ions as a corrosion inhibitor for aluminum alloy in different environment (Udoh et al., 2019; Abirami et al., 2021). To the best of our knowledge the modification of cerium into nano-hybrid (MOF) form as a combination of inorganic and organic parts has not been explored for CO2 corrosion mitigation. Herein, we demonstrate the synergy of the cerium nano-hybrid organic ligand and the metal ions/cluster. Interestingly, a positive output was obtained which is in accordance with other MOFs based corrosion inhibitors highlighted. The synthesis, characterization and performance evaluation was successfully achieved using series of advanced analytical procedures to unveil the uniqueness of Ce-MOF in CO2 corrosion mitigation.

Further, we considered the optimization of the empirical data using machine learning (ML) model. ML based algorithms are suitable for complex system prediction and optimization to make ultimate prediction on the new data (Anadebe et al., 2020). It can be described as a method of approximating a function that maps out the input with respect to the outputs. The use of ML to appraise the efficiency of an inhibitor is gradually gaining attention in corrosion inhibition studies and pipeline integrity assessment for the oil and gas sector. Therefore, the current study utilized the ML while focusing on a fascinating synthesis route for the preparation of cerium based zeolitic imidazole framework nanomaterial as a corrosion inhibitor for X65 pipeline steel in CO2-saturated 3.5% NaCl solution and its evaluation using detailed empirical, DFT/MD-simulation and machine learning model approach.

Analytical grade chemicals such as Methanol (CH4O), 2-Methylimidazole (C4H6N2) 99%, cerium nitrate hexahydrate (Ce (NO3)20.6 H2O), ethanol (C2H5OH) were purchased and utilized as obtained without further treatment.

0.8 M 2-methylimidazole was dissolved in 100 ML of methanol and stirrer for 10 min (solution A). Ce (NO3)20.6 H2O (0.05 M) was dissolved in 100 ML of methanol and stirred for 10 min (solution B). Gently, the 2-methylimidazole solution was added drop wise into cerium nitrate solution and the mixtures were continuously agitated at room temperature for 1 h with a magnetic stirrer to aid the reaction and obtain a homogenous phase. This results in the formation of a milk-colored Ce-MOF powder precipitate as shown in Scheme 1. The obtained precipitate was washed five times with ethanol and deionized water using a centrifuged at 4500 rpm and further dried at room temperature.

API 5 L X65 steel was used in this study in line with the previous report (Anadebe et al., 2022a). The samples were cut from high grade oil and gas pipeline steel and machined into 1.3 × 1.3 cm dimensions. Each test coupon was properly polished with emery sheets of various grades (#200 to #1200 grit size). The polished samples were immediately rinsed with deionized water after being cleaned with acetone to avoid further dissolution prior to corrosion test according to ASTM practice.

The electrochemical measurement was carried out using (Auto-lab PGSTAT302) with FRA module in a three electrodes system. The reference and counter electrodes were made of Ag/AgCl, and platinum foil, respectively, while the X65 steel is the working electrode. A working and constant pH of 3.8 was reached by first purging 3.5% NaCl solution with CO2 for 1 h and continuously during the corrosion test. The X65 steel with mirror finishing was submerged into the CO2 brine solution and the acidic solution was maintained throughout the experiment by purging the CO2 gas. After that, an impedance study was conducted with a sinusoidal 10 mV amplitude perturbation in the frequency range of 100 kHz to 0.01 Hz. By using a scan rate of 0.2 mVs-1 and a potential range of − 250 mV to + 250 mV with respect to the OCP, then the polarization study was investigated.

BIVOVIA materials studio version 7.0 software was employed to study the molecular structure and adsorption orientation of the Ce-MOF on the steel surface after immersion in CO2 saturated solution. The electronic structures of Ce-MOF were set on using the density functional theory approach. Details of the molecular simulation as previously reported was used (Anadebe et al., 2022a; Abdallah et al., 2022).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Machine learning via adaptive neuro-fuzzy inference system (ANFIS)
ce:section.ce:para: ANFIS is a machine learning system operating on the principles of fuzzy logic to construct rule system by entering the data of the prediction model to obtain accurate membership functions. However, since ANFIS is an adaptive network model that operates on fuzzy logic and neural programming, the model is designed to have inputs, hidden layers and an output parameter. The aim of this soft computing is to integrate such machine learning methodology as neural network and fuzzy system for optimum inhibition efficiency predictions (Anadebe et al., 2022b).

The crystallinity and phase purity of the precursors and synthesized hybrid inhibitor (Ce-MOF) was scrutinized via a powder X-ray diffraction, Fig. (1ai-1aiii). All the diffraction peaks observed were sharp and clear, indicating a highly ordered structure phase (Cao et al., 2019). The XRD pattern of 2-methylimidazole shows orthorhombic structure, while Ce-MOF displays strong diffraction peaks at 2 θ values of 28.3 °, 36.3 °, 47.5 ° and 56.3 ° corresponding to (111), (200), (220) and (311) planes respectively (Zeng et al., 2016). Fourier transform infrared (FTIR) spectra of 2-methylimidazole, pure Ce-MOF and adsorbed Ce-MOF on the steel surface are presented in Fig. (1bi-1biii). The 2-methylimidazole spectrum exhibited a broad band with high intensity at 2640 cm−1 which is within the wave number range of 3244–2238 cm−1 usually attributed to the hydrogen bonds existing between the N-H groups present in pyrrole and pyridinic. The resonance mechanism between the N-H…H bending and stretching vibration of N-H band is clearly seen at 1848 cm−1 (Lashgari et al., 2021). In the pure Ce-MOF spectrum (Fig. 1bii), the peak number appearing at 3418 cm−1 represent stretching vibration of the hydroxyl group which justifies that the water molecules have been physically adsorbed (Cheng et al., 2020). The characteristics bands within the region of 1616 –1552 and 1432–1368 cm−1 are attributed to the vibration vassy (-COO-) and vsym (-COO-) of the carboxylate ions (Cheng et al., 2020). It is imperative to note that the peak with wave length of 527 cm−1 corresponds to the stretching vibration of Ce-O bond. All the observed peaks indicate that the Ce-metal has been coordinated with the imidazole ligand, showing that Ce-MOF has been successfully synthesized. After corrosion inhibition study, close scrutiny of the functional groups involved in the inhibition mechanism was carried out on the inhibited samples via FTIR as shown in Fig. (1b iii), evidently, almost all the peaks observed in the pure Ce-MOF were present in the coated sample, but with significant shift in the peak numbers of asymmetric and symmetric vibration of the coordinated carboxylate ions which is attributed to their synergistic activity.

The morphology of Ce-MOF was investigated by FE-SEM as shown in Fig. (2). The observed image revealed that the surface of Ce-MOF is porous in nature and have a spherical wool shape interwinging each other (Payra and Roy, 2021). In addition, little aggregation was seen at high magnification. Furthermore, high resolution transmission electron microscopic analysis also supported the morphologies as shown in Fig. 3 at different magnifications.

The HR-TEM images show the formation of aggregated nanoparticles. Also at 20 nm range a random morphology of individual crystallites with an average size of 5–7 nm was observed in line with previous report (Sridharan et al., 2020). In same vein, the circular pattern obtained from the SAED imaging visibly reveals the crystalline planes of Ce-MOF as shown in Fig. 3c, this observation strongly conforms to the XRD results previously discussed. In addition, the presence of each element that makes up the Ce-MOF has been proven, as evidenced in Fig. 3f-3i such as C, N, O and Ce which shows the purity of Ce-MOF sample. The expected elements were successfully distributed in the frame work. The HAADF-STEM shows the uniformity of the expected elements, and it is evident from Fig. 3d and 3e that the synthesized Ce-MOF is free from scums. Furthermore, in view to examine the Ce-MOF to determine its surface elements, oxidation states and binding energy, XPS analysis was employed as shown in Figs. 4a-4d. The survey scan of Ce-MOF confirms the presence of C 1 s, N 1 s, O 1 s and Ce 3d elements. The high-resolution spectra were also considered important for wider understanding. In Fig. 4b, the C1s region evidenced three distinctive peaks at 283. 3, 284.7, and 287.5 eV corresponding to C-C, C-O-C, and C-CO respectively (Abdelhamid et al., 2020). In Fig. 4c, the N1s core level spectrum exhibited a peak at the binding energy of 398.2 eV for C-N. Fig. 4d shows that the core level spectrum of O 1 s could be fitted by two peaks at binding energies of 530.4 and 528.3 eV representing CO and C-O which are attributed to the oxygen component on the carboxylate groups of the imidazole linker. In Fig. 4e, the core-level spectrum of Ce3d revealed the existence of a mixed valence state (Ce3+/Ce4+) in the Ce-MOF, justifying the presence of redox-active cerium atoms in the frame work (Abdelhamid et al., 2020; Sangeetha and Krishnamurthy, 2020). The peaks centered at 881.8, 899.5, and 915.8 eV can be attributed to Ce4+, while the peak region at 886.8 and 905.2 eV are the known characteristics peaks of Ce3+.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Open circuit potential (OCP) and polarization study
ce:section.ce:para: In electrochemical systems, when a piece of metal is subjected to an electrolyte, disorganization of an equilibrium system will occur and consequently result in the movement of ions, generation of charging current, followed by charge separation and formation of a double layer between the metal and electrolyte interface. As a result of these phenomena, there will be varying potential until the value of the corrosion potential of the specific metal is attained at the equilibrium state. In this case, OCP is measured for 1 h, within this time, the resting potential mode and stability of the system was achieved for X65 steel in CO2 solution. As observed in Fig. 5a, a slight rise in potential at the onset of the experiment and eventually it stabilizes as indicated by the straight lines. The trend of these curves signifies the absence of a protective coating layer at the beginning and the evident of its eventual formation. As the time and reaction progresses, there is a significant shifting mechanism of the potential to a more positive side as a result of this protective layer formation with a systematic increase in the Ce-MOF concentrations. The inhibited curves exhibited a different reaction trend from the blank curve which rather tends more negative as seen in Fig. 5a.

The polarization plots for X65 steel subjected into simulated CO2 solution (blank) and at varying Ce-MOF dose is presented in Fig. (5b). The vital parameters such as corrosion potential

It is evident from Fig. 5b that the polarization curves in the presence of varying Ce-MOF concentrations tends more to the positive potential relative to the blank curve, showing superior anodic inhibition strength of Ce-MOF. The observed uniform decrease of the corresponding corrosion current densities with increasing Ce-MOF concentration is attributed to the formation of anodic protective layer on the electrode surface (Sasikumar et al., 2015; Zhang et al., 2021b). A compound can be termed as either anodic or cathode inhibitor when the deviation in the
(
E
c o r r
)
value between the inhibited and the uninhibited system is above 85 mV (Yan et al., 2020; Mousavi et al., 2011). In our case, the variation in the
(
E
c o r r
)
values obtained between the inhibited and uninhibited system were less than this threshold value of 85 mV, which justifies Ce-MOF as a mixed type inhibitor with predominantly anodic protection activity (Onyeachu et al., 2019; Tian et al., 2017; Zhang and Zhao, 2017). Invariably, Ce-MOF controls the anodic and cathodic partial reactions. However, the cathodic curves apparently displayed a poorly define Tafel region. It is widely reported that Tafel slope can be valid if the curve exhibits linearity for at least 1 decade of current on the Tafel region, otherwise, a valid Tafel parameter can be obtained from one side that exhibit linear region (Obot et al., 2019). Thus, the
(
I
c o r r
)
values were extrapolated from the Tafel region on the anodic arm of the curve. Also, the slight variations in the values of the anodic Tafel region in the presence of Ce-MOF may be ascribed to the adsorption of chloride ions from the electrolyte or Ce-MOF molecules on the X65 steel surface, or due to some redox process that necessitate the Fe inhibitor complexes on the metal active sites (De Souza and Spinelli, 2009). Furthermore, the organic linker (2-methylimidazole) was equally tested at maximum concentration of Ce-MOF_0.2 wt% to study its effect prior to its synergy with a metal ions/cluster. Evidently, the curve change to a more negative direction and a low inhibition efficiency was recorded, but in hybrid form (inorganic-organic), the inhibition efficiency was increasing with respect to Ce-MOF concentration because the metal ions/cluster enhances the sacrificial efficacy while the organic linker forms the protective film layer on the steel surface. Maximum inhibition efficiency of 95% was achieved at Ce-MOF_0.15 wt%. Thus, showing that Ce-MOF molecules could perform better at medium concentration relative to its high concentration of 0.2 wt%. The observed trend could be due to congestion at a high concentration which might reduce the movement of the active sites of the Ce-MOF molecules or desorption as already stated (Chukwuike et al., 2019).

EIS is a non-destructive method for proper evaluation of corrosion inhibition properties of an inhibited system (Cherrad et al., 2022). As shown in Fig. 6a, the Nyquist plot exhibited a well depressed semicircular shape at low frequency corresponding to charge transfer mechanism. The nature of semicircular shapes as observed is due to the frequency dispersion mechanism, fractal geometry, surface inhomogeneity and coarseness of the electrode surface (Zhang et al., 2020a, 2020b). In addition, the blank system exhibited an inductive effect at low frequency region due to the formation of deposits as intermediates products species (Wei et al., 2017). The extended size of each inhibited semicircular plot increases systematically with an increase in Ce-MOF concentration compared to the blank system, showing that the addition of Ce-MOF can impede the ingress of the corrosion agents and thus improving the inhibition efficiency through the formation of organic-inorganic protective film layer on the steel surface. Also, the observed trend in the impedance modulus Fig. 6b increased as the Ce-MOF concentration increases, demonstrating that a layer of organic and inorganic film exists over the steel and consequently a decline in the corrosion rate. The phase angle plot as shown in Fig. 6c evidenced a wide and broad shift with an overlap in the high and medium frequency region revealing the existence of two relaxation time constant and compactness of Ce-MOF protective film layer (Cen et al., 2019). The observed wide shift in the phase angle with a systematic increase in Ce-MOF could be attributed to the organic-inorganic protective film layer on the steel surface, thus modifying the electrode interfacial structure. Proper simulation of the electrochemical kinetics was carried out and the parameters were presented in Table 2. To obtain a good fitting of the data set, a known circuits system was adopted as shown in Fig. 6i-6ii.

The ‘n′ factor correlates to surface heterogeneity with respect to the blank and inhibited steel surfaces respectively (Zhu et al., 2021). Furthermore, close scrutiny of the plots evidenced that the organic linker (2 methyl imidazole) exhibited almost similar trend with the blank system, showing poor inhibition performance in the studied environment even at the maximum concentration studied. But when in combination with a metal ion/cluster the charge transfer resistance increased in each inhibited system with respect to Ce-MOF concentration, showing superior activity of Ce-MOF molecules on X65 steel in CO2 solution. The largest diameter observed in each plot was at Ce-MOF_0.15 wt% which corresponds to the best inhibition efficiency of 95% for Ce-MOF. The overall inhibition efficiency was calculated using the relationship in Eq. 3 as previously documented (Anadebe et al., 2022a).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Surface characterization of the uninhibited and inhibited metal surface
Field emission scanning electron microscopy FE-SEM/EDAX and atomic force microscopy
ce:section.ce:para: FE-SEM image Fig. 7 were obtained from a polish X65 steel surface, blank steel subjected to CO2 solution and the one protected by Ce-MOF. As shown in Fig. 7a, a neat and uniform abrading line was observed since no corrosion has yet occurred. Fig. 7b shows the steel surface in contact with CO2 solution in the absence of Ce-MOF. As observed, the X65 steel surface was severely attacked by the carbonic acid resulting in the formation of visible cracks, deep pits (Singh et al., 2019). The nature of the cracks and pits could be attributed to the dissolution of ferrite phase of the steel. Fe, O and C were the top elements detected on the X65 steel surface in this case. On the contrary, Fig. 7c reflects a different surface morphology (smooth surface) when subjected to CO2 solution in synergy with Ce-MOF (Zhang et al., 2020a, 2020b). The Ce-MOF molecules covered the steel surface. The observed coating layer was intact and dense, thus sub planting or suppressing the ingress of carbonic acid to the steel surface. The expected elements from Ce-MOF (Fe, C, N, O and Ce) were detected by EDAX in the case of inhibited surface. In addition, the cross sectional view of the corrosion deposits on the blank sample and inhibitor layer on the inhibited sample was considered. The blank sample Fig. 7g exhibited a severe corroded edge having unstable scales and deposits corresponding to iron-oxides (Zhang et al., 2012). As opposed to that, (Fig. 7h) shows evidently the protected edge with a distinctive arrangement of the inhibitor layer grown on the steel which confirm the stable coating state of Ce-MOF on the steel surface. Also, to explore the topography of the corroded and inhibited metal surface, atomic force microscopy was used. In (Fig. 7i), very rough and mountain like peaks of corrosion deposits were observed due to aggressive attack after immersion in the blank CO2 solution (Murmu et al., 2019). In the case of the steel surface with Ce-MOF a uniform topology was seen in Fig. 7j as already discussed in the FESEM.

ce:section.ce:section-title: X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy of the inhibited X65 steel surface
ce:section.ce:para: The XPS survey scan as shown in Fig. 8 evidenced the existence of Ce-MOF elements on the inhibited steel. The C 1 s spectrum could be deconvoluted into three peaks at 284.8, 285.3, and 288.5 eV, possibly corresponds to C–H/C–C, CO/CN and FeCO3 (Singh et al., 2017). The N 1 s spectrum comprises of two peaks at 397.4 eV which corresponds to CN bonds, while 399.6 eV is attributed to the –NH2 groups in the Ce-MOF and coordination between the N atoms of –NH2 groups and Fe (N-Fe). The O 1 s spectrum has four distinctive peaks at 529.6, 531.3, 530.7 and 533.5 eV, which correspond to the Fe oxides,-OH, CO, and C–O/CO3 2-. For Fe 2p3/2 spectrum, the peaks appeared at 706.6, 711.1, 714.4 and 724.7 eV are linked with iron in the range of Fe, FeOOH, FeCO3 and Fe2O3. It is clear that Ce 3d exhibited broad peaks which indicate the presence of multiple oxidation states for Ce. The Ce 3d plot is fitted in spin–orbit doublets of Ce 3d5/2 and Ce 3d3/2. The peaks at 885.4 eV and 906.5 eV are attributed to the satellite peaks of Ce4+ 3d5/2 and Ce4+ 3d3/2 respectively, which possibly is originating from Ce–O bonding in Ce-MOF.

Quantum chemical technique is a useful tool in elucidating electronic structure and reactivity of a compound. In this study, the interaction between the active sites in the Ce-MOF molecules and X65 steel surface was explored via DFT calculations and MD-simulation model. Figs. 9a-9d evidenced the optimized structure, electron density, HOMO and LUMO spatial distributions of Ce-MOF. According to the Frontier molecular orbital hypothesis, E
HOMO relates to electron-donating capability of a molecule and E
LUMO indicates the tendency of a molecule to accept electrons (Asadi et al., 2019). Invariably, a molecule with a high E
HOMO value and a low E
LUMO value is considered a good corrosion inhibitor. In addition, (ΔE) is a vital concept called energy-gap to determining the reactivity of Ce-MOF molecule in the adsorption onto metallic surface (Iroha et al., 2021). As the (ΔE) values decreases, the reactivity site of the HOMO and LUMO increases resulting to good interaction between the reacting species (Sarkar et al., 2022). As evidenced in Table 3 the low value of (ΔE) is in line with higher affinity of the Ce-MOF to get adsorbed on the metal surface. As seen in the spatial distributions, the HOMO level was obviously located on the N atoms in the framework. Some quantum chemical parameters, such as absolute electronegativity (χ), global hardness (η), global softness (σ) and fraction of electron transferred from the inhibitor molecule to the Fe surface, (ΔN) were computed as follows:

The adsorption mechanism of the investigated compound on the steel surface in aqueous environment was further explored using MD simulations. Molecular dynamics simulation approach has been used to appraise inhibitor molecules at atomic scale (Pareek et al., 2019). These approaches are regarded as efficient simulation tools to obtain in depth molecular understanding of processes associated corrosion inhibition studies. The side and top view of the equilibrium configuration for adsorption of the Ce-MOF on X65 steel in aqueous environment are presented in Fig. (9e–f). As the molecular simulation progresses, the position of the Ce-MOF tends gradually towards the steel surface with a flat adsorption disposition as illustrated in Fig. (9e–f). It is believed that in aqueous state there will be more competition between the water and Ce-MOF molecules for steel surface. Thus, Ce-MOF must sub-plant the water molecules to achieve a better protected surface coverage. As evidenced from the results, the system have somewhat negative values of interaction energy which implies that Ce-MOF is associated with stable, strong bond and spontaneous adsorption on the X65 steel surface (Fe 110) (Obot et al., 2013). Evidently, the flat adsorption trend can be attributed to the addition of a more electropositive metal (Ce) into the organic matrix which enhances the efficiency of the inhibitor while the organic linker (2 methyl imidazole) forms the protective layer on the steel surface. Overall, there is good match between the theoretical and the experimental findings.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Machine learning prediction via ANFIS modeling
ce:section.ce:para:
Fuzzy logic systems have been designed and used as an advanced machine learning technique for multiple domains to solve complex nonlinear problems (Li et al., 2021). ANFIS is operating based on the principles of simulation of human brain. Applicability of machine learning has increased in recent times in both research and industrial level with more significant attention in the oil and gas sector for pipeline integrity assessment and as a risk management tool for diverse predictions (Abbas et al., 2018; Keprate et al., 2017). The ANFIS model depends on some important parameters such as input variables, number of layers, number of rules and the output variable; it can exist as a multi input-multi output (MIMO) or multi input-single output (MISO) modeling. Prior to system analysis and prediction, the response data was de-normalized to obtain an error free system prediction. In the context of this study, 80% data was used for training and 20% was adopted for testing. The hybrid algorithm enhances the robustness of the ANFIS model for these grouping. From the input variables, the number of constant membership function for output response was four and consequently resulted in obtaining four inference rule system as presented in the model design Fig. 10a. The proposed ANFIS model has five inputs variables (operating conditions) and a single neuron denoting the inhibition efficiency. The input neuron is attached to one neuron showing one membership function in each case. The hidden neuron is related to the output neuron where individual neuron in the hidden layer is fixed to the neuron in the output node. The membership function is in a triangular geometry and the output distribution is further de-fuzzified (Anadebe et al., 2022a; Mensah et al., 2020). The 3D-surface viewer plots were studied. As presented, each plot shows undulating curves with three distinctive color regions. The yellow color region denotes the high performing areas, while medium and low areas are indicated by the light blue and deep blue zones, respectively (Anadebe et al., 2022b). The graphical correlation between time and inhibitor concentration is shown in Fig. 10b. It is seen that the allocated time is adequate and the gradual increase in Ce-MOF dosage resulted in better inhibition efficiency due to the Ce-MOF active sites available for adsorption on the steel surface. In Fig. 10c, the pH changes the stability of the adsorbed coating through adsorption or desorption, which affects how a metal behaves in terms of corrosion. Also as shown in Fig. 10d, at low temperature range with an increase in Ce-MOF, better inhibition efficiency was attained due to less acquisition of energy influence by the corrosive solution that may cause desorption of the Ce-MOF active molecules from the steel surface. In Fig. 10e, as temperature and pH increases the rate of corrosion will increase because low pH value accelerates corrosion. Herein, the temperature ranges do not affect the Ce-MOF surface attachment significantly. Furthermore, the ANFIS predicted data was plotted against experimental data to ascertain the correlation, evidently as presented in Fig. 10 f a good linear graph with R2 value of 1 (unity) was obtained using Eqs. (8–9).

The statistical norms of obtaining such value strongly confirms the model evaluation fitness, thus it has made it a preferred machine learning model for solving complex engineering task. As presented in Table 4, ANFIS model systematically predicted the experimental data with optimum value of 94.9% at Ce-MOF_0.15 wt% which strongly complements the experimental data.

Corrosion inhibition of X65 pipeline steel in CO2 environment by Ce-MOF can be discuss elaborately on the basis of molecular adsorption mechanism as presented in Fig. 11. The heteroatoms such as N, and O in the organic ligands are freely filled into the unoccupied 3d orbitals due to the lone pair of electrons and coordination bond existing between the heteroatoms and the Fe surface (Chen et al., 2020). The X65 steel surface has positive charge which enhances attachment of Ce-MOF on the basis of donor acceptor interactions, thus substituting the water molecules from the Fe surface and sharing of electrons between the nitrogen atoms in the framework. The heteroatoms are the centers of negative charges and with their planar л-orbitals on the Fe surface which can impede further metal dissolution (Etaiw et al., 2011). The theoretical modeling evidenced a flat adsorption mechanism on the Fe surface. This phenomenon results in the interaction of unshared pairs of electrons of N atoms and the π electrons with the vacant d-orbitals of the Fe atom. The inhibition trend justifies that Ce-MOF is attached on the Fe surface via the organic framework parallel to the Fe surface.

Following conclusions can be drawn from this work:
•
Ce-MOF was successfully synthesized via a wet chemical method for efficient inhibition of the corrosion of X65 steel in CO2 environment.
•
Electrochemical studies evidenced that Ce-MOF can inhibit the cathodic and anodic partial reactions of X65 steel corrosion in CO2 environment. This observation implies that Ce-MOF is a mixed type corrosion inhibitor with anodic predominance.
•
The surface analysis via XPS, FE-SEM/EDAX and AFM techniques confirm the presence of Ce-MOF molecules on the metal surface thus we correlate the remarkable corrosion protection observed to them.
•
The MD-simulation concept suggests a stable and flat adsorption disposition of Ce-MOF molecules on the X65 steel surface in studied environment which could be responsible for high surface coverage that helped to promote corrosion mitigation of the synthesized compound, the results are in good agreement with the experimental findings.
•
The multi-input single output machine learning model predicted the nonlinear variables affecting the output response with high degree of accuracy. Thus, ANFIS model can be employed for real time pipeline safety predictions.
